# SPI Master (Mode 0)

## Description
This project implements an SPI (Serial Peripheral Interface) Master in Verilog HDL, operating in Mode 0 (CPOL=0, CPHA=0). Unlike UART, SPI is synchronous — the Master generates and shares a clock line with the Slave, enabling full-duplex communication (simultaneous send and receive) over 4 wires.

## Inputs
* mosi_data[7:0] : byte to transmit to the slave
* MISO : serial data line, receiving bits from the slave
* start : pulse to begin one SPI transaction
* clk, reset : system clock and reset

## Outputs
* SCLK : serial clock, generated by this module
* MOSI : serial data line, transmitting bits to the slave
* SS : slave select, active LOW
* miso_data[7:0] : byte assembled from bits received on MISO
* busy : HIGH while a transaction is in progress

## Working
Implemented as a 3-state FSM (IDLE, TRANSFER, DONE), combined with two edge-triggered shift processes:
* MOSI is driven on the falling edge of SCLK (MSB first)
* MISO is sampled on the rising edge of SCLK (MSB first)
* SCLK itself is generated internally, only toggling while a transaction is active, idling LOW between transactions (per Mode 0 / CPOL=0)

A single shared bit_count register tracks progress across all three processes (SCLK generation, MOSI shift-out, MISO shift-in), ensuring both directions of data transfer stay synchronized to the same 8-bit boundary.

## Bug Fixed During Verification
An initial version exhibited a trailing one-cycle delay and one extra unwanted SCLK pulse after the 8th bit was transferred. This was caused by the FSM's "transfer complete" check relying on a registered value (bit_count) that was itself derived through a chain of dependent clocked blocks (clk -> SCLK -> bit_count), introducing lag. Fixed by deriving a combinational wire (transfer_done = bit_count==8) and using it directly in both the SCLK generator and the FSM, eliminating the relay delay.

Verified with two back-to-back 8-bit transactions, confirming byte-perfect MOSI transmission and MISO reception in both directions simultaneously.
